title: Fix misleading ParseError status when Claude Code produces no output
description: When Claude Code implementer exhausts all turns but produces no file changes, the pipeline reports status=ParseError. This is misleading — the real issue is 'no output produced' or 'task too complex for turn budget'. Should have a distinct status like NoOutput or TurnBudgetExhausted. Seen in canary run with refactor-agent-selection-logic task (27 turns, 0 files changed). Affects crates/eng-org/src/pipeline.rs and agents/claude_code.rs.

title: Aggressive Task Decomposition
description: Force smaller, more focused sub-tasks by tightening decomposition rules and reducing per-task token budgets. Target: atomic sub-tasks that touch 1-2 files, require 1-2 edits, complete in 4-5 tool turns. Higher success rate through simplicity.

title: Tighten planner decomposition thresholds
description: Update planner system prompt decomposition conditions: decompose when task touches 3+ files (was 4+), requires 4+ steps (was 5+), or total affected lines exceed 400 (was 500). Sub-tasks MUST include files_likely_affected array. Each sub-task should touch at most 2 files (1 source + 1 test). Update planner prompt and tests.

title: Sub-task file scope enforcement in pipeline
description: Add pipeline-level validation for decomposed sub-tasks: reject sub-tasks whose files_likely_affected contains more than 2 files. When the planner produces sub-tasks with too many files, fail with PlanFailed and message instructing further decomposition.

title: Reduce TaskSize::M token budget to 60K
description: Reduce TaskSize::M max_tokens from 120K to 60K to force tighter task scoping. Adjust TaskSize::L to 90K (was 120K). Update budget estimation thresholds accordingly. Validate with canary batch first. 60K is a pragmatic step between current 120K and ADR-8's 30K target.

TQM — Self-improvement as first-degree architecture
description:
After GLITCHLAB runs a batch, it analyzes outcomes, detects failure patterns, and generates beads to fix itself. Dogfooding as architecture.

## Principle
GLITCHLAB should improve itself as it runs. When it observes patterns like decomposition loops, scope creep, or model degradation, it should create beads to fix its own bugs. TQM (Total Quality Management) as architecture.

## Insertion points
- Post-orchestrator loop: aggregate OrchestratorResult + Vec<TaskRunResult>, detect patterns, emit beads
- In orchestrator loop: between tasks, check for systemic failure patterns (restart intensity)
- In pipeline: OutcomeContext already captures structured failure data — wire it into planner prompt on retry

## Pattern types to detect
- Decomposition loops (>N decompositions in sequence)
- Scope creep (>N ScopeTooLarge obstacles)
- Model degradation (>N ParseFailure/ModelLimitation from same model)
- Stuck agents (>N max_stuck_turns hits)
- Test flakiness (>N TestFailure from same file)
- Architect rejection rate (>N% ArchitectRejected)
- Budget pressure (tasks hitting max_dollars_per_task)
- Provider failures (ExternalDependency for same service)
